response.justification: Strong software engineer with proven systems expertise, but the role requires deep hardware architecture experience that is absent from the resume. The candidate's background is entirely software-centric (web, backends, microservices, distributed systems) without demonstrated SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture analysis, or AI accelerator optimization expertise that defines the Principal Performance Architect role.
strengths: Extensive performance optimization background with low-latency systems at Dunnhumby and sophisticated database tuning
Senior-level systems architecture experience spanning microservices, cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK), and distributed systems
Proven learning agility across diverse technical domains and strong software craftsmanship practices
concerns: No experience with SoC/IP modeling, chip design, or hardware description languages critical to the core role
Missing GPU/accelerator architecture expertise and deep neural network workload optimization skills
Current AI-agent infrastructure work appears to focus on MCP protocols for developer tooling, not AI compute optimization

key_factors.factor: SoC/IP modeling and hardware design expertise
GPU architecture and AI accelerator optimization experience
Performance analysis and systems optimization background
